seoTitle: Build a Cookie Consent Popup in Webflow
metaDescription: Design a GDPR-friendly cookie consent banner in Webflow using native elements and interactions, without a heavy third-party widget.
intro: Third-party cookie consent widgets often slow down page speed and clash with a site's custom design. This guide shows how to build a compliance banner directly in Webflow using native container elements, interactions, and a small custom code snippet to remember user choices.

steps.heading: Build the banner layout
steps.body: Open the Add Elements panel, drag in a container box, and set it to a fixed position for your banner.

steps.heading: Add your text and buttons
steps.body: Insert privacy text and create accept and settings buttons styled to match your site's branding.

steps.heading: Configure the interaction
steps.body: Open the Interactions panel and build a custom click trigger that smoothly hides the banner when a visitor clicks accept.

steps.heading: Add a hide animation
steps.body: Set up an animation sequence so the popup dismisses cleanly rather than disappearing abruptly.

steps.heading: Paste the compliance script
steps.body: Add your cookie consent script inside the project's custom code settings so the site remembers a visitor's choice, then test and publish.

faq.question: Do I need a third-party plugin to add a cookie banner in Webflow?
faq.answer: No, a native container, buttons, and interactions can handle the visual banner, paired with a small custom code script for compliance tracking.

faq.question: Where does the cookie tracking script go?
faq.answer: Custom code snippets are pasted inside the project settings so the site can remember whether a visitor has already responded.

faq.question: Will the banner display correctly on mobile?
faq.answer: Test your banner across responsive breakpoints in preview mode to confirm it reads well on smaller screens.

faq.question: Can I let visitors change their cookie preferences later?
faq.answer: Yes, a settings link alongside the accept button can reopen the preference options for visitors who want to adjust their choice.
